entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A nutri-cereal containing high levels of Fe and Zn, characterized by a protogynous nature and high cross-pollination.

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A cereal crop used for comparison with pearl millet regarding micronutrient levels.

entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: A cereal crop used for comparison with pearl millet regarding micronutrient levels.

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: A cereal crop used for comparison with pearl millet regarding micronutrient levels.

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop used for comparison with pearl millet regarding micronutrient levels.

entities.name: HETEROSIS
entities.description: The superiority of F1 hybrids over their parents, extensively studied in pearl millet.

entities.name: PROTOGYNOUS NATURE
entities.description: A biological characteristic of pearl millet that makes it highly cross-pollinated.

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC-NUCLEAR MALE-STERILE SOURCES
entities.description: Genetic sources in pearl millet used to develop hybrids and enhance productivity.

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of agricultural output, which can be enhanced by hybrid development or reduced by disease.

entities.name: BLAST DISEASE
entities.description: A major disease in pearl millet causing significant yield loss, up to 62 percent.

entities.name: PYRICULARIA GRISEA
entities.description: The fungal pathogen that causes blast disease in pearl millet.

entities.name: DISEASE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop hybrid to withstand or resist the effects of blast disease.

entities.name: REGIONAL AGRICULTURAL RESEARCH STATION, VIJAYAPUR
entities.description: The site where the disease resistance breeding programme was initiated.

entities.name: SCREENING
entities.description: The process of evaluating and selecting parents and cross combinations for hybrid development.

entities.name: EVALUATION
entities.description: The process of assessing the performance of parents and cross combinations.

entities.name: SELECTION
entities.description: The process of choosing the best parents and cross combinations for breeding.

entities.name: VPMH-7
entities.description: A high-yielding, blast-disease-resistant pearl millet hybrid released in 2019.

entities.name: ICMA 94555
entities.description: An early flowering A1 cytoplasm based male-sterile line used as a parent for VPMH-7.

entities.name: CPRT 112
entities.description: A good restorer line used as a parent for VPMH-7.

entities.name: MEAN YIELD
entities.description: A measure of productivity, recorded as significantly higher in VPMH-7 compared to the national check.

entities.name: GHB 558
entities.description: The national check variety used as a benchmark for comparing the performance of VPMH-7.

entities.name: ZONE 3 OF KARNATAKA
entities.description: The specific geographic region where VPMH-7 was tested and notified for commercial cultivation.

entities.name: FARM TRIALS
entities.description: A testing method used to evaluate the performance of the VPMH-7 hybrid.

entities.name: LARGE SCALE DEMONSTRATIONS
entities.description: A testing method used to evaluate the performance of the VPMH-7 hybrid on a broader scale.

entities.name: EARLY MATURITY
entities.description: The characteristic of maturing in 80-85 days, making a crop suitable for late planting.

entities.name: MULTIPLE CROPPING SYSTEM
entities.description: An agricultural system where multiple crops are grown in sequence, facilitated by early maturing varieties.
